differential_diagnoses: Duodenal perforation (penetrating peptic ulcer) with retroperitoneal abscess and secondary thrombophlebitis
Lemierre's syndrome variant (Streptococcus anginosus) with deep space infection
Crohn's disease with enterorenal fistula and abscess
Tuberculosis (disseminated) involving mediastinal nodes and abdomen
next_steps: Urgent upper endoscopy to evaluate the duodenum for ulceration or fistula
Echocardiography (transthoracic or transesophageal) to rule out endocarditis
Stool examination for ova and parasites (specifically Strongyloides)
Repeat blood cultures to monitor for clearance of bacteremia
Continue systemic anticoagulation for renal vein/IVC thrombosis
Consult Interventional Radiology for potential drainage of the pararenal fluid collection if identified on imaging
Review clinical status for escalation to surgical consultation if evidence of contained duodenal perforation persists
